scipy.reason: target_tool: eigvalsh for symmetric eigenvalues, minimize for scalar optimization, cdist for pairwise distances, entropy for information measures — all load-bearing for this isolation probe.

B_minimize_positive.note: minimize((x-3)^2, x0=0) converges to x≈3.0 within 1e-5

A_eigvalsh_negative.note: eigvalsh on non-symmetric matrix silently uses lower triangle; result may differ from full eig — caller must ensure symmetry

B_minimize_negative.note: Non-smooth |x|: BFGS may report success but x may not be exactly 0; success flag checked

C_cdist_negative.error: XA and XB must have the same number of columns (i.e. feature dimension.)
C_cdist_negative.note: cdist with mismatched dimensions correctly raises ValueError

B_minimize_boundary.note: minimize started at x0=3.0 (already at minimum); converges immediately with minimal iterations

C_cdist_boundary.note: cdist of point with itself = 0.0 exactly

D_entropy_boundary.note: entropy([]) returns nan or 0 without crash
